subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Bloodborne Pathogens
description: A short lesson to help you apply Bloodborne Pathogens.

hook.text: HIV, Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C all live in blood. Contact with someone else's blood is never casual.

buildup.text: Bloodborne pathogens spread through contact with infected blood via needlesticks, wounds, or mucous membranes.

discovery.text: Hepatitis B is 100 times more infectious than HIV—it survives outside the body for 7 days.

twist.text: Universal precautions treat ALL blood as potentially infectious—because you can't tell by looking.

climax.text: Gloves, eye protection, and proper needle disposal prevent nearly all occupational exposures.

punchline.text: All blood. Always. Universal precautions assume every drop is infectious.

quiz.question: What does 'universal precautions' mean?
options: Treat all blood as potentially infectious regardless of the source
Only use precautions with known infected patients
